item.title: Brian Eno
item.description: Era1971–present (primary period: 1973–1983)InstrumentSynthesizer, tape, treatments, voiceGenresAmbientArt rockArt popElectronicListenRYM·Credits·Apple Music·Spotify On Roxy Music’s first two records there is an instrument the credits can’t quite name: the rest of the band, fed through Brian Eno’s sy...

item.title: Dance
item.description: RYM On Valentine’s Day 1970, David Mancuso needed rent money and threw a party at his loft on 647 Broadway: about a hundred guests, two dollars and fifty cents at the door with food and punch included, a sound system assembled like a hi-fi obsessive’s living room (because it was one), no liquor lic...

item.title: Love Saves the Day
item.description: BeforeIn 1970 New York, dancing to records meant the discotheque: a licensed commercial format behind a velvet rope, imported from France in the mid-'60s.AfterOne rent party, $2.50 at the door and nothing for sale, became a weekly institution: the host, the sound system, the invited floor.

item.title: CBGB
item.description: The Bowery bar with the two rules — original music only, haul your own gear — whose door-deal economics and stolen-then-replaced sound system built punk's founding room.

item.title: The Loft
item.description: David Mancuso's invitation-only rent party: no bar, no license, no closing time — the audiophile room dance music still copies.

item.title: (1978) Ambient 1: Music for Airports
item.description: Brian EnoReleasedFebruary 1979 (the sleeve’s notes are dated September 1978)LabelPolydor / E.G.

item.title: Lawrence — Love Saves the Day
item.description: Author: Tim Lawrence Title: Love Saves the Day: A History of American Dance Music Culture, 1970–1979 Year: 2003 Type: Academic book (Duke University Press) On Valentine’s Day 1970, David Mancuso threw a rent party in his loft at 647 Broadway with a hundred or so guests and a homemade invitation read...

item.title: George Martin
item.description: Era1950s–1998 (primary period: 1962–1970)GenresPop rockMerseybeatPsychedelic popArt rockBaroque popPsychedelic rockKey artistsThe BeatlesListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify George Martin signed the Beatles because nobody else would, and the records they made together over the next eight years changed what...
